author: tuyen4818
content:
I have a branch for lodestar
- 1) and 2) are done. 2) could be improved with divide and conquer
- 3) at fork transition I only pre-verified builder deposit signatures, can enhance to also pre-verify same pubkey validator deposits. Not sure how to deal with at a block
- 4) a TODO

author: tuyen4818
content:
but in general I want to avoid a caching solution for state-transition if we can, it may not be scanned through spec tests
pre-gloas, every state-transition is a pure function, passing a spec test is a big deal
now we break that, that shifts the testing effort to devnet and I don't think we can cover all scenarios in devnets, testing effort becomes huge at devnet
all clients can pass spec tests and there is still uncertain at devnets, bc each client has its own caching solution
in the long term, I don't think we want we to keep thinking about cache in state-transition spec

author: stefanbratanov
content: that's nice idea! will do that

author: tersec
content: in particular, long-lived caches also can break (become corrupted, not handle some forked scenario, etc) in surprising/hard-to-debug ways. Of course the answer is not to make mistakes, but empirically caches have proven across several CLs to be sources of bugs

author: potuz
content: what do you mean by long lived cache here? this is stateful, you can keep it in the state itself

author: potuz
content: you just need to remember which signatures in the state were valid/invalid

author: tersec
content: well, depends how things are set up. Right now in Nimbus we don't really have a state + random stuff bundle

author: tersec
content: targeted optimizations for specific things (those big SSZ lists)

author: tersec
content: and not keeping a lot of states at all

author: potuz
content: this is only useful for head state

author: tersec
content: well, there can be multiple possible heads, and a reorg means that something that wasn't a head can become a head

author: potuz
content: Sure, and the only one that can affect this is reorgs across an epoch boundary, which you already need to be able to handle, and I'm pretty sure you keep caches of the shuffling for example precisely because of this issue

author: tersec
content: yes, Nimbus does keep such shuffling caches
